description: Grace Nakamura (patient identifier: BAH000025) is a 58-year-old female who was admitted 3 days ago following an emergency appendectomy. She is now presenting with signs of a surgical site infection including wound erythema, purulent discharge, and persistent fever. She reports a history of allergic urticaria from Penicillin (moderate severity) which has never been formally documented in the system. Access the Bahmni system at https://localhost/bahmni/home (login: superman / Admin123). Conduct her post-operative follow-up encounter: record her current vitals (Temperature 38.4 degrees Celsius, Pulse 96 bpm, Blood Pressure 132/84 mmHg, Respiratory Rate 20 per minute, SpO2 97 percent, Weight 64 kg), formally document the Penicillin allergy with reaction type Urticaria and severity Moderate, prescribe Ciprofloxacin 500mg orally twice daily for 10 days as a Penicillin-safe antibiotic for the surgical site infection, order follow-up investigations Complete Blood Count and C-Reactive Protein to monitor infection markers, record diagnoses of Appendicitis (primary, confirmed) and Wound Infection (secondary), and write a clinical progress note of at least 200 characters documenting the post-operative assessment, antibiotic selection rationale, and follow-up plan.
